fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to new mexico, pharmacy technician compact new mexico, new mexico pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address: New Mexico Board of Pharmacy, 2550 Cerrillos Road, 2nd Floor, Santa Fe, NM 87505

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in New Mexico.

steps.title: 1. Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you are at least 18 years of age, a high school graduate or equivalent (GED), and have successfully completed a Board-approved pharmacy technician training program OR are currently certified by the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA).

steps.title: 2. Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Schedule a fingerprinting appointment through IDEMIA for a state and national criminal history screening. Visit the New Mexico Board of Pharmacy Criminal Background Check page (rld.nm.gov/boards-commissions/boards/board-of-pharmacy/forms-applications/criminal-background-check/) for instructions and the required service code (24BG5029).

steps.title: 3. Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the New Mexico Regulation & Licensing Department Portal (portal.rld.nm.gov). Once registered and logged in, select the Board of Pharmacy and complete the 'Pharmacy Technician Initial Application'.

steps.title: 4. Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the required non-refundable initial application fee and initial registration fee through the online portal. The total combined fee is $100.

steps.title: 5. Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of high school graduation/GED and evidence of Board-approved training or PTCB/NHA certification, directly through your online application on the RLD Portal.

steps.title: 6. Await Board Review
steps.description: The New Mexico Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application and criminal background check results. You will be notified of your application status through the portal or via mail.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: New Mexico offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the New Mexico Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in New Mexico by submitting a new application to the New Mexico Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any New Mexico-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in New Mexico by submitting a new application to the New Mexico Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any New Mexico-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in New Mexico
hero.dek: Apply online through the New Mexico Regulation & Licensing Department (RLD) Portal (portal.rld.nm.gov). First, complete your criminal background check, then create an account, select the Board of Pharmacy, and submit your Pharmacy Technician Initial Application.

official_links.portal_name: New Mexico Regulation & Licensing Department Portal
official_links.registration_steps: New users must create an account by registering on the portal, providing personal details, and confirming their registration. Once logged in, navigate to the Board of Pharmacy section and select the 'Pharmacy Technician Initial Application' to begin.
